false_coverage_claims.finding: REQ-006-AC-2 (summary ≤20 logical items per dispatch contract) had no test case. Resolved in iteration 2 self-fix: TEST-070 added to MOD-005 to verify all DISPATCH CONTRACT blocks declare this constraint.

REQ-008-AC-3 (deprecation warning display during migration window) had no test case. TEST-031/032 only test isWithinMigrationWindow return value, not the actual warning output. Resolved in iteration 2 self-fix: TEST-069 added to MOD-004.

REQ-002-AC-2 requires both gemini AND opencode experimental entries in the registry. Only gemini is specifically tested as experimental (TEST-014). The opencode experimental entry has no dedicated test case.

missing_cases.finding: No test for empty source file (zero-length content, no placeholders). renderSkillFile on empty input is a valid boundary; current tests assume non-empty source.
No test for a source file that is entirely a conditional block rendering to empty string on a given platform. The validateOutput behavior on zero-length conditional output is unspecified.
getPlatform for 'opencode' (experimental) has no test. REQ-002-AC-2 requires both gemini and opencode experimental entries. Only gemini is covered by TEST-014.
No test for asymmetric file sets in diff validation: prompts/codex/ contains files with no counterpart in generated output, or vice versa. TEST-044/045 only test matched file sets.

No test for mixed scenario: some managed files + some modified files + some unrelated files all present simultaneously in the same directory. Each category is tested in isolation. Combined scenario may expose ordering or interaction bugs.

Expected versionsRemaining=0 uses exact equality for a value that could be negative (floor of 0). Without a floor(0) specification this is ambiguous, though the exact-equality assertion is acceptable as a reasonable contract definition.

issues.finding: FIXED in iteration 2. Original source string contained `<%= user.name %>` which eta would evaluate and throw UNDEFINED_VARIABLE before reaching the CRITIC_PATH substitution — making the expected_output unreachable. Replaced with the properly escaped eta syntax `<%%=user.name%%>` which outputs literal `<%=user.name%>` without evaluation. Expected output is now a concrete string, fully deterministic and executable.

expected_output uses 'excludes' key — a negative assertion annotation embedded as if it were a real output field. An implementation would return an array of platform objects. The test framework must interpret 'excludes' as a negative assertion rule. This is non-standard and requires test harness documentation to execute correctly.

CLARIFIED in iteration 2. The ears_ref REQ-008-AC-1 is retained but an ears_ref_note field has been added to document the distinction: this test verifies source file authoring discipline (commands/ marker presence) as the authoring-side precondition for AC-1's runtime detection behavior. MOD-004 TEST-024~027 cover the runtime detection. The conceptual separation is now explicit.
FIXED in iteration 1 (confirmed in iteration 2 re-read). ears_ref was corrected from REQ-001-AC-1 to REQ-001-AC-3. scanSourceSkills is part of the init execution flow, which is what AC-3 describes.

PROP-004 ('all generated files derivable from commands/ source + PlatformAdapter mapping') is semantically redundant with PROP-003 (idempotency). If the same inputs always produce the same output, derivability from source is already implied. The property does not add new violation-detection capability beyond PROP-003.

PROP-007 references INV-005 which has requirement_refs: []. The property tests an internal implementation guideline (≤35 line files not split) with no backing user requirement. Added for structural symmetry rather than user-facing correctness.

No generator specification provided. Any constant-output function trivially satisfies a determinism property. Should specify: generate arbitrary source strings with <%=VAR%> tokens and arbitrary variable maps, run renderSkillFile twice with identical inputs, assert byte-identical outputs.

No generator specification provided. The property should explicitly state the scanning step: discover all <%=VAR%> tokens across commands/, then assert each active platform's placeholderMap contains every token. Without this, an implementation might only check a statically enumerated list.

Violation criterion 'business logic' is approximated by '5+ consecutive non-instruction lines' heuristic. This is a stated mechanical proxy, not a formal definition. False positive rate may be high for complex markdown content. Acceptable as a lint-level check but cannot be treated as a formal correctness property.

Self-containment is proxied by keyword presence in first 20 lines ('Feature Mode', 'mode=feature', 'mode=greenfield'). A file could contain these keywords in a comment without having functional branching logic. Acceptable as a structural presence check, but does not verify semantic correctness of the mode-branching behavior.
Idempotency property is well-formed and executable: run installAllPlatforms twice, diff output directories, assert identical content. No significant weakness.

patterns_found.evidence: coverage_summary declares requirements_uncovered: [] and invariants_uncovered: [] with both coverage_guards set to true. Before iteration 2 fixes, REQ-006-AC-2 and REQ-008-AC-3 were genuinely uncovered despite the requirement IDs appearing in covered lists. Coverage was counted at requirement-ID granularity without verifying AC-level completeness. This overstates the actual test coverage.

Across all 6 modules, dispatch behavioral tests (what actually happens when a subagent is dispatched, what context isolation is verified, what happens when dispatch fails mid-execution) are absent. The feature's primary goal is reliable dispatch isolation, yet tests verify only static structural properties of skill files (line counts, annotation presence, keyword grep). The dispatch execution path — success and failure — is not exercised by any test.

TEST-043 conflates source file authoring discipline (commands/ files have nopilot-managed markers) with LegacyMigrator runtime detection (detecting those markers in ~/.claude/commands/ during init). Clarified with ears_ref_note in iteration 2 but the structural distinction remains relevant for implementers.

PROP-007 ('≤35 line files not split') references INV-005 with requirement_refs: []. Added for structural completeness (each module has property tests) rather than because it protects a user-facing requirement.

fixes_applied: Added TEST-049 (EJS compatibility boundary test) to MOD-001 — original had non-executable input; iteration 1 added a version but with a still-problematic source string containing unevaluable `<%= user.name %>`
Added TEST-050 (e2e scan for placeholder comment in source files) to MOD-001 covering REQ-001-AC-2
Added TEST-051 (modified file default-N behavior) to MOD-004 covering REQ-008-AC-4
Added TEST-052 (modified file explicit-y behavior) to MOD-004 covering REQ-008-AC-4
Added TEST-053 through TEST-068 to MOD-005 covering REQ-003/004/005/009/010 AC-level behavioral checks
Fixed TEST-018 ears_ref from REQ-001-AC-1 to REQ-001-AC-3

TEST-049: Replaced non-executable EJS source string (`<%= user.name %>` causes UNDEFINED_VARIABLE before CRITIC_PATH substitution) with properly eta-escaped source `<%%=user.name%%>` that outputs literal `<%=user.name%>` without evaluation. Expected output is now a deterministic concrete string.

TEST-043: Added ears_ref_note field to MOD-005 explicitly documenting the authoring-discipline vs runtime-detection distinction. ears_ref REQ-008-AC-1 retained as it is the precondition for that AC to function.
Added TEST-069 to MOD-004: verifies init stdout/stderr includes deprecation warning when isWithinMigrationWindow returns active=true. Covers REQ-008-AC-3.
Added TEST-070 to MOD-005: verifies all DISPATCH CONTRACT blocks declare summary ≤20 logical items constraint. Covers REQ-006-AC-2.

trend_evaluation.detail: Iteration 1 reduced block count from ~12 to 5. Iteration 2 resolved all 5 remaining blocks. Warn count is stable at 11 (no new warns introduced). Cap not reached.

detail: All 5 block-severity findings from this iteration have been resolved: (1) TEST-049 non-executable input fixed with correct eta escape syntax; (2) TEST-043 concept conflation clarified with ears_ref_note; (3) REQ-006-AC-2 coverage gap addressed by TEST-070; (4) REQ-008-AC-3 coverage gap addressed by TEST-069; (5) TEST-018 ears_ref was already fixed in iteration 1. Remaining 11 warn-level findings are documented above — they are improvable but do not prevent correct implementation. Notable residual concerns: dispatch behavioral tests are entirely absent across all modules (the test suite verifies structural properties of skill files rather than runtime dispatch behavior, which is inherent to the nature of Markdown skill files and not fixable within the tests artifact alone); opencode experimental entry has no dedicated test; several property tests lack generator specifications.
